records.ety: [F., fr. L. ode, oda, Gr. 'w,dh` a song, especially a lyric song, contr. fr. 'aoidh`, fr. 'aei`dein to sing; cf. Skr. vad to speak, sing. Cf. Comedy, Melody, Monody.]

defs: A short poetical composition proper to be set to music or sung; a lyric poem; esp., now, a poem characterized by sustained noble sentiment and appropriate dignity of style.
quotes: Hangs odes upon hawthorns and elegies on brambles.
O! run; prevent them with thy humble ode, And lay it lowly at his blessed feet.
